description: Self-learning hooks that automatically maintain workspace context after meaningful work

hooks.prompt:
You are evaluating whether Claude Code should stop or first update workspace context files.

Hook input: $ARGUMENTS

RULES:
1. If stop_hook_active is true, respond {"ok": true} — updates are already in progress or complete.
2. If the last_assistant_message indicates only Q&A, greetings, clarifications, trivial fixes, or brief responses (under ~100 words of substance), respond {"ok": true}.
3. If meaningful work was completed (code changes, data analysis, workflow execution, research with findings, multi-step task completion), respond {"ok": false} with the reason below.

When returning ok: false, use this exact reason:
"Before finishing, review your work and update workspace context. Do this efficiently in a single pass:
1. SKILLS: If you discovered new patterns, fixes, or workflow improvements, update the relevant skill files in skills/
2. COMPANY: If new business context was discovered, update .claude/docs/COMPANY.md or create/update linked reference files
3. TODO: Update .claude/docs/TODO.md — mark completed tasks, add new ones identified during work
4. HISTORY: Append a one-line summary to .claude/docs/HISTORY.md, then create a detailed session file in .claude/docs/history/ named YYYY-MM-DD-HH-MM-SS-descriptive-title.md
5. CLAUDE.md: Only update if fundamental workspace context changed (new tools, conventions, or structure)
Create any missing files or directories. Follow .claude/rules/documentation.md if it exists. Be concise."

Respond with JSON only: {"ok": true} or {"ok": false, "reason": "..."}
